mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-decline periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, it signals severe labor market slack and deteriorating consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0% over a 60-day horizon, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return direction in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during periods of active Fed rate changes, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining housing starts indicate a contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following three consecutive monthly declines in housing starts, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside a flat or rising federal funds rate signals tightening real rates and reduced corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power or flips direction when unemployment exceeds 6.5%, or quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.
